optional_params.name: method
optional_params.description: Method to use: 'zscore', 'modified_zscore', 'iqr', or 'percentile' (default: 'zscore')

optional_params.name: threshold
optional_params.description: Threshold for determining outliers: For zscore/modified_zscore: number of standard deviations; For iqr: multiplier of IQR (values outside Q1-threshold*IQR and Q3+threshold*IQR); For percentile: values below/above percentile thresholds (default: 3.0)

optional_params.name: window
optional_params.description: Window size for rolling outlier detection (if None, use entire series)

optional_params.name: time_column
optional_params.description: Time column to sort by before calculations (optional)

optional_params.name: group_columns
optional_params.description: Columns to group by before calculations (for panel data, optional)

outputs.description: Function that detects outliers in an AnomalyPipe using statistical methods.

detect_statistical_outliers.description:
Detect outliers using statistical methods

Parameters:
-----------
columns : str or List[str]
    Column(s) to analyze for outliers
method : str, default='zscore'
    Method to use: 'zscore', 'modified_zscore', 'iqr', or 'percentile'
threshold : float, default=3.0
    Threshold for determining outliers:
    - For zscore/modified_zscore: number of standard deviations
    - For iqr: multiplier of IQR (values outside Q1-threshold*IQR and Q3+threshold*IQR)
    - For percentile: values below/above percentile thresholds
window : int, optional
    Window size for rolling outlier detection (if None, use entire series)
time_column : str, optional
    Time column to sort by before calculations
group_columns : List[str], optional
    Columns to group by before calculations (for panel data)

Returns:
--------
Callable
    Function that detects outliers in an AnomalyPipe

optional_params.name: method
optional_params.description: Method to use: 'residual', 'prediction_interval', 'changepoint' (default: 'residual')

optional_params.name: model_type
optional_params.description: Model to generate expected values: 'ewm', 'sarimax', 'decomposition' (default: 'ewm')

optional_params.name: threshold
optional_params.description: Threshold for determining anomalies (std deviations for residuals, prediction intervals) (default: 3.0)

optional_params.name: window
optional_params.description: Window size for rolling model fitting (default: 30)

optional_params.name: seasonal_period
optional_params.description: Number of periods in a seasonal cycle (if None, will be estimated)

optional_params.name: group_columns
optional_params.description: Columns to group by before calculations (for panel data, optional)

optional_params.name: datetime_format
optional_params.description: Format string for parsing dates if they are not already datetime objects (optional)

outputs.description: Function that detects contextual anomalies in an AnomalyPipe.

detect_contextual_anomalies.description:
Detect contextual anomalies by comparing actual values with expected values

Parameters:
-----------
columns : str or List[str]

model_type : str, default='ewm'
    Model to generate expected values: 'ewm', 'sarimax', 'decomposition'
threshold : float, default=3.0
    Threshold for determining anomalies (std deviations for residuals, prediction intervals)
window : int, default=30
    Window size for rolling model fitting
seasonal_period : int, optional
    Number of periods in a seasonal cycle (if None, will be estimated)
group_columns : List[str], optional
    Columns to group by before calculations (for panel data)
datetime_format : str, optional
    Format string for parsing dates if they are not already datetime objects

Returns:
--------
Callable
    Function that detects contextual anomalies in an AnomalyPipe

required_params.name: columns

optional_params.name: n_estimators

optional_params.name: group_columns

detect_collective_anomalies.description:
Detect collective anomalies using machine learning methods on multiple features

Parameters:
-----------
columns : str or List[str]
    Column(s) to analyze for anomalies (multiple columns for multivariate analysis)
time_column : str
    Column containing the time/date information
method : str, default='isolation_forest'
    Method to use: 'isolation_forest', 'lof' (Local Outlier Factor)
window : int, default=30
    Window size for rolling analysis
contamination : float, default=0.05
    Expected proportion of anomalies in the data
n_estimators : int, default=100
    Number of estimators (for tree-based methods)
group_columns : List[str], optional
    Columns to group by before calculations (for panel data)
datetime_format : str, optional
    Format string for parsing dates if they are not already datetime objects

Returns:
--------
Callable
    Function that detects collective anomalies in an AnomalyPipe

forecast_and_detect_anomalies.description:
Forecast time series and detect anomalies based on forecast errors

-----------

seasonal_period : int, optional
    Number of periods in a seasonal cycle (if None, will be estimated)

    Columns to group by before calculations (for panel data)
datetime_format : str, optional
    Format string for parsing dates if they are not already datetime objects

--------
Callable
    Function that forecasts and detects anomalies in an AnomalyPipe

required_params.name: methods
required_params.description: List of method configurations, each with: - 'name': Method name (e.g., 'statistical_outliers', 'contextual_anomalies') - 'params': Parameters for the method

optional_params.name: ensemble_method
optional_params.description: How to combine results: 'any' (union), 'all' (intersection), 'majority', 'weighted' (default: 'any')

optional_params.name: output_suffix
optional_params.description: Suffix for output anomaly flag columns (default: '_ensemble_anomaly')

outputs.description: Function that applies multiple anomaly detection methods in an AnomalyPipe.

batch_detect_anomalies.description:
Apply multiple anomaly detection methods and combine results

Parameters:
-----------
columns : str or List[str]
    Column(s) to analyze for anomalies
methods : List[Dict[str, Any]]
    List of method configurations, each with:
    - 'name': Method name (e.g., 'statistical_outliers', 'contextual_anomalies')
    - 'params': Parameters for the method
ensemble_method : str, default='any'
    How to combine results: 'any' (union), 'all' (intersection), 'majority', 'weighted'
output_suffix : str, default='_ensemble_anomaly'
    Suffix for output anomaly flag columns

Returns:
--------
Callable
    Function that applies multiple anomaly detection methods in an AnomalyPipe
